title: Personal Narrative of Travels to the Equinoctial Regions of America, During the Year 1799-1804 — Volume 2

quoteFirst: "_October 1st._--We had a heavy pull against tide, and arrived at Sarawak
quoteLast: We had eaten nothing since breakfast at 8; and we had to sit and talk, and drink tea and smoke, till 8 in the evening; then dinner was announced, and we retired to the private apartments--my poor men came willingly too! The table was laid _à l'Anglaise_, a good curry and rice, grilled fowls, and a bottle of wine. We did justice to our cheer; and the rajah, throwing away all reserve, bustled about with the proud and pleasing consciousness of having given us an English dinner in proper style; now drawing the wine; now changing our plates; pressing us to eat; saying, 'You are at home.' Dinner over, we sat,
title:
The Expedition to Borneo of H.M.S. Dido
For the Suppression of Piracy
author: Keppel, Henry, Sir, 1809-1904; James, Rajah of Sarawak, 1803-1868
